Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.freemarker

Class FreemarkerMixin

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.freemarker.FreemarkerMixin
public class FreemarkerMixin extends Object
Mixin that wires Apache FreeMarker view-rendering onto any Juneau REST resource.

Compose into a host resource via @Rest(mixins=FreemarkerMixin.class); the host then:

  1. Gains a default mount at /freemarker/* that serves raw FreeMarker templates from the importer's classpath by asking the configured Configuration for the named template and rendering it with an empty data model (raw render path; callers who want attributes use FreemarkerView from a typed handler instead).
  2. Picks up FreemarkerViewRenderer automatically via the mixin's @Rest(responseProcessors=...) declaration, so @RestOp-method return values of type FreemarkerView render through the FreeMarker engine without any additional wiring.
Composition example (microservice):

@Rest(path="/app", mixins=FreemarkerMixin.class) public class AppResource extends RestServlet { @Bean FreemarkerMixin freemarker() { return FreemarkerMixin.create() .basePath("/templates/") .templateSuffix(".ftlh") .build(); } @RestGet("/hello/{name}") public View hello(@Path String name) { return FreemarkerView.of("hello").attr("name", name); } }

Configurable mount path:

The default mount /freemarker/* can be overridden via the SVL variable ${juneau.freemarker.path:freemarker} — set via system property (-Djuneau.freemarker.path=views), environment variable (JUNEAU_FREEMARKER_PATH=views), or Config key (juneau.freemarker.path = views) to change the runtime mount without subclassing. Resolution happens once at RestContext construction time (SVL resolution in @RestOp(path)).

Mixin-only deployment:

This resource is designed for composition via @Rest(mixins=...). The mount path is pinned at the op level by @RestGet(path="/{juneau.freemarker.path:freemarker}/*") on render(java.lang.String, org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestRequest, org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestResponse); a class-level @Rest(paths=...) declaration would be silently ignored under the mixin pattern (see @Rest(paths) Javadoc).

Choosing a Configuration:

The juneau-rest-server-view-freemarker module ships only org.freemarker:freemarker in provided scope. No configuration bean is bundled. The bridge resolves a Configuration at first use from three sources, in order:

  • User-supplied bean: register a @Bean freemarker.template.Configuration (Spring) or BasicBeanStore.put(freemarker.template.Configuration.class, cfg) (microservice). Spring Boot's spring-boot-starter-freemarker autoconfigures one out of the box; the bridge picks it up via BeanStore.getBean(Configuration.class).
  • Bridge default: when nothing is registered, the bridge constructs a default Configuration anchored on a classpath resource root derived from basePath ("/templates/" becomes ClassLoaderTemplateResolver-equivalent prefix "/templates"). The default pins IncompatibleImprovements to the bridge-tested minor version (Configuration.VERSION_2_3_34), sets DefaultEncoding to UTF-8, uses HTMLOutputFormat so HTML escaping is the natural target, and applies TemplateUpdateDelayMilliseconds per the cacheTemplates flag (production-safe by default; users opt into hot-reload via cacheTemplates(false)).

When no FreeMarker engine is on the classpath, the renderer surfaces FreemarkerViewRenderer.NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC naming the missing dependency.

Template suffix:

FreeMarker's API does not expose a configuration-level resolver suffix — the literal template name is what cfg.getTemplate(...) sees. The templateSuffix(...) builder knob fills the gap: when non-blank, the bridge appends the configured suffix to template names that don't already end with it (idempotent), so callers can write FreemarkerView.of("hello") or request /freemarker/hello and have it resolve to hello.ftlh on the classpath.

.ftl vs .ftlh:

FreeMarker auto-selects HTML escaping by file extension: .ftlh templates emit HTML-escaped output; .ftl templates emit raw output. For HTML responses, prefer .ftlh so a future attribute-binding change can't introduce an XSS regression.

Multiple base paths:

A host with /templates/ and /admin/templates/ template trees registers two FreemarkerMixin beans, each mounted via a subclass declaring its own op-level @RestGet(path=...) override and its own basePath. See the FreemarkerMixin_MockRest_Test test in juneau-integration-tests for the canonical pattern.

OpenAPI surface:

The greedy /* handler is not API-meaningful and is excluded from generated Swagger / OpenAPI specs via @OpSwagger(ignore=true).

See Also:
  • FreemarkerView
  • FreemarkerViewRenderer
  • FreeMarker View Support
  • REST Server — Mixins and Multi-Mount Paths
Since:
10.0.0

    static final String

    Default base path applied when no FreemarkerMixin.Builder.basePath(String) call has been made.

    Default template-cache flag — true (production-safe).
    static final String
    DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX
    Default template suffix — empty (literal template names, no implicit suffix).

  • Constructor Details

    • FreemarkerMixin

      public FreemarkerMixin()
      No-arg constructor — uses DEFAULT_BASE_PATH as the base path, DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX as the template suffix, and DEFAULT_CACHE_TEMPLATES as the cache flag.

      The mixin walk falls back to this constructor when the BeanStore does not have a registered FreemarkerMixin bean.

    • FreemarkerMixin

      protected FreemarkerMixin(FreemarkerMixin.Builder builder)
      Builder constructor.
      Parameters:
      builder - The builder. Must not be null.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static FreemarkerMixin.Builder create()
      Creates a new builder.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • getBasePath

      public String getBasePath()
      Returns the base path under which template resources are resolved.
      Returns:
      The base path. Never null.

    • getTemplateSuffix

      public String getTemplateSuffix()
      Returns the configured template-name suffix.
      Returns:
      The template suffix. Never null.

    • isCacheTemplates

      public boolean isCacheTemplates()
      Returns the template-cache flag.
      Returns:
      The cache flag.

    • applyTemplateSuffix

      public String applyTemplateSuffix(String name)
      Appends getTemplateSuffix() to name if not already present (idempotent).
      Parameters:
      name - The template name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The template name with the configured suffix appended (if applicable).

    • resolveConfiguration

      public freemarker.template.Configuration resolveConfiguration(RestRequest req)
      Resolves the active Configuration via the shared FreemarkerDispatcher worker.
      Parameters:
      req - The current REST request.
      Returns:
      The active FreeMarker configuration. Never null.

    • render

      public void render(@Path("/*") String path, RestRequest req, RestResponse res) throws IOException, BasicHttpException
      [GET /freemarker/*] — render a raw template through the FreeMarker engine.

      The @Path("/*") String path captures the multi-segment trailing remainder (e.g. a request for /freemarker/about.ftlh matches the mount with path = "about.ftlh"). Delegates to the shared FreemarkerDispatcher worker (path-traversal hardening, suffix application, configuration resolution, and render).

      Missing template surfaces as the engine's own TemplateNotFoundException (HTTP 500 from the renderer's catch-all); missing engine surfaces as FreemarkerViewRenderer.NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC.

      Parameters:
      path - The trailing path segment after /freemarker/ (the template name relative to the configured base path; configured suffix appended if missing).
      req - The current REST request.
      res - The current REST response.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the underlying response writer fails.
      BasicHttpException - On boundary violation (403), missing engine (500), or render failure (500).
